data_flow_trace.original: GOVERNANCE calls JWKManager.setPatches(patches) with a large Patch[] — requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE) passes
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: setPatches is external, gated only by requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE) at L162. The calldata Patch[] has no length cap; L168-173 iterates patches.length and pushes every element into storage _patches, then calls _regeneratePatchedJWKs() at L178 in the same transaction.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE is the only gate; no bound check on patches.length before the copy loop or before regeneration.

data_flow_trace.original: _patches is replaced (delete + push loop); _regeneratePatchedJWKs runs inline (initial bloat absorbed by governance tx)
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: delete _patches (L165) wipes prior state; a bounded-per-iter push loop (L168-173) copies the calldata array in; _regeneratePatchedJWKs() is invoked synchronously at L178 inside the governance tx. The governance tx itself may be funded with arbitrary gas (no SYSTEM_CALLER gas cap here), so it does not OOG trivially.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Governance supplies gas; no contract-side safety valve.

data_flow_trace.original: SYSTEM_CALLER invokes NativeOracle.record(sourceType=1, sourceId=keccak256(issuer), nonce, payload, gasLimit) with gasLimit bounded by consensus policy
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: record is gated to SYSTEM_CALLER (L82), advances nonce (L85 via _updateNonce), then calls _invokeCallback(..., callbackGasLimit) at L90. callbackGasLimit is an opaque parameter set by the off-chain consensus engine — contract treats it as fixed/bounded input.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: SYSTEM_CALLER-only; gasLimit is passed through verbatim to the callback.

data_flow_trace.original: NativeOracle._invokeCallback dispatches to JWKManager.onOracleEvent which reaches _regeneratePatchedJWKs — cost O(I²+P·I) per rotation
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: NativeOracle._invokeCallback (L314) does try IOracleCallback(callback).onOracleEvent{gas: gasLimit}(...). JWKManager.onOracleEvent passes version check (L135), updates _issuerVersions (L140), upserts observed (L143), then unconditionally calls _regeneratePatchedJWKs() at L148. _regeneratePatchedJWKs (L334-359) does: _clearPatchedState (L336; O(I) storage deletes), a loop over _observedIssuers calling _upsertPatchedProvider (L339-348) where each call invokes _insertSortedIssuer (L386) — a linear scan + shift of _patchedIssuers, O(I) per insert × I inserts = O(I²); plus a loop over every patch (L351-356) where _upsertJWKToPatched may call _insertSortedIssuer (L550) — O(P·I) worst case. Net ≈ O(I² + P·I) matching the claim.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Runs on every JWK rotation for any issuer, not just the one being rotated. No early-exit or incremental update path.

data_flow_trace.original: With P (or I) large, _regeneratePatchedJWKs OOGs the fixed callback gasLimit; try/catch stores raw payload and emits failure path
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The try/catch at L314-325 catches any revert (including OOG) and returns true, so NativeOracle.record then stores the raw payload at L93-100 and emits CallbackFailed (L323) + DataRecorded. The callback's state mutations (version bump, observed upsert, patched regen) all revert. One nuance: after an OOG, the outer record still has at least 1/64 gas remaining per EIP-150, so the fallback store and event-emission path typically succeeds. The original description accurately reflects the fallback behavior.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Requires that the O(I²+P·I) regeneration actually exceeds the callback gasLimit supplied by consensus. This is an offchain constant; the contract provides no upper-bound assurance.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: none of substance; minor: the fallback emits CallbackFailed (not a dedicated rotation-failure event).

data_flow_trace.original: _issuerVersions not advanced; subsequent rotations repeat OOG until governance clears patches
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Because onOracleEvent reverts (OOG inside _regeneratePatchedJWKs at L148), all its state writes are rolled back — _issuerVersions[issuerHash] stays at the pre-rotation value and observed state is unchanged. Meanwhile NativeOracle._updateNonce (L85, L257-270) already persisted the nonce increment outside the try, so nonces advance but JWK state does not. The patches array remains until GOVERNANCE calls setPatches([]) — which itself re-runs _regeneratePatchedJWKs one final time with the governance-sized gas budget.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Recovery requires a governance tx with enough gas to run the final regen with P=old_large + the clear; on a congested chain this may need multiple attempts as the description notes.

reasoning_chain: All six claimed steps are present in code at a623eab. (1) setPatches has zero length validation (JWKManager.sol:159-179). (2) It inlines a full regeneration. (3-4) onOracleEvent also calls _regeneratePatchedJWKs on every rotation (L148); complexity is provably O(I²+P·I): the observed-copy loop invokes _insertSortedIssuer (L428-459) which is a scan+shift, giving I² over I issuers; the patch-apply loop can add I-width shifts per UpsertJWK patch. (5) NativeOracle.sol:314-325 has the try/catch-OOG-fallback exactly as described. (6) The nonce advances regardless (L85) while callback state reverts, so subsequent rotations keep OOGing with the same bloated patches. No cap, no pagination, no incremental-update shortcut exists anywhere in the contract. The severity rating Medium is justified: governance-triggered (not attacker-triggered), governance-recoverable, but silent and targets a consensus-critical output (keyless-account auth).

key_evidence.excerpt: function setPatches(Patch[] calldata patches) external override { requireAllowed(SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE); delete _patches; for (uint256 i; i < patches.length;) { _patches.push(patches[i]); ... } emit PatchesUpdated(patches.length); _regeneratePatchedJWKs(); }
key_evidence.why_matters: Demonstrates the only gate is governance authorization; no patches.length cap and regeneration is inlined in the same tx.

key_evidence.excerpt: function _regeneratePatchedJWKs() internal { _clearPatchedState(); for (uint256 i; i < _observedIssuers.length;) { ... _upsertPatchedProvider(issuerHash, observed.issuer, observed.version, observed.jwks); ... } for (uint256 i; i < _patches.length;) { _applyPatch(_patches[i]); ... } emit PatchedJWKsRegenerated(_patchedIssuers.length); }
key_evidence.why_matters: The full-rebuild-from-scratch pattern. Each _upsertPatchedProvider calls _insertSortedIssuer → O(I) per issuer → O(I²); each _applyPatch/UpsertJWK on a new issuer is also O(I). Confirms O(I²+P·I).

key_evidence.excerpt: function _insertSortedIssuer(bytes[] storage issuers, bytes memory issuer) internal { ... for (uint256 i; i < len;) { if (_compareBytes(issuer, issuers[i]) < 0) { insertIdx = i; break; } ... } issuers.push(); for (uint256 i = len; i > insertIdx;) { issuers[i] = issuers[i - 1]; ... } issuers[insertIdx] = issuer; }
key_evidence.why_matters: Sort-insert scans + shifts the storage issuers array; combined with _compareBytes this is the quadratic inner operation.

key_evidence.excerpt: function onOracleEvent(...) external override returns (bool shouldStore) { requireAllowed(SystemAddresses.NATIVE_ORACLE); ... _issuerVersions[issuerHash] = version; _upsertObservedProvider(...); emit ObservedJWKsUpdated(...); _regeneratePatchedJWKs(); return false; }
key_evidence.why_matters: Every JWK rotation triggers a full O(I²+P·I) regen, regardless of which single issuer changed. This is the DoS amplifier.

key_evidence.excerpt: _updateNonce(sourceType, sourceId, nonce); ... try IOracleCallback(callback).onOracleEvent{ gas: gasLimit }(...) returns (bool callbackShouldStore) { ... } catch (bytes memory reason) { emit CallbackFailed(sourceType, sourceId, nonce, callback, reason); return true; }
key_evidence.why_matters: Shows (a) nonce is committed before callback, so rotations keep being consumed; (b) callback revert is swallowed, preserving the stuck state silently; (c) the contract always passes the caller-supplied gasLimit, offering no floor/ceiling safety.

counterargument: One could argue this is not a real vulnerability because: (a) setPatches is governance-only, so the attacker must be governance itself — effectively a governance footgun, not an external DoS. (b) P in practice is bounded by the number of emergency JWK overrides governance actually needs, likely O(10s). (c) If the callback OOGs, the raw payload is still stored by NativeOracle (L93-100), so in principle another consumer could read _records[1][sourceId][nonce] and recover off-chain. (d) Governance can always call setPatches([]) to clear. However these don't defeat the issue: getJWK/hasJWK read _patchedProviders only (L191,L211), not _records, so stored-raw payload is dead to consumers; governance-only trust doesn't remove the bug class (governance operator error or social-engineered patch list), and the claim's metric is a reachable stuck-state not an attacker-controlled one; and setPatches([]) itself must succeed on a congested chain at the same O(P·I) cost. So the medium severity stands.
